pr.title: Working through various data labels and finish non-.data labels
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly data-label cleanup across asm/data.s, asm/sdata.s, and asm/bss.s. The PR split previously coarse .skip regions into many address-accurate global labels, replaced raw pointer constants such as 0x8045A090 and 0x804D3EF8 with lbl_* references, and converted at least one large block of hex-encoded string data into explicit .asciz strings with labels and .balign 4. The title says this was intended to work through various data labels and finish non-.data labels; there were no PR body or review comments to provide more intent.
what_changed: In asm/bss.s, large anonymous .skip spans were subdivided into many .global lbl_* symbols at exact addresses, for example splitting lbl_80459DF0's 0x3F0 span into labels including lbl_80459E68, lbl_80459EC8, lbl_80459F28, lbl_80459F88, lbl_8045A000, lbl_8045A090, and others.

In asm/bss.s, additional BSS regions around 0x80480DA8, 0x804831A8, 0x80487810, 0x80490960, 0x80497758, and 0x804D6872 were split into smaller labeled .skip entries so references can target symbols instead of interior offsets.
In asm/data.s, many .4byte raw addresses were replaced with symbolic labels, e.g. 0x8045A090 became lbl_8045A090 and 0x804D3EF8/0x804D3F00 became lbl_804D3EF8/lbl_804D3F00.
In asm/data.s, raw address pointer tables for data and sdata addresses were symbolized, including lbl_804D4010 through lbl_804D4048 and lbl_804D40D8 through lbl_804D4130.

In asm/data.s, a large block of hex-encoded THP filename bytes was converted into explicit labeled .asciz strings such as "GmRegendAdventureCaptain.thp", "GmRegendAdventureDonkey.thp", and related Adventure/Allstar movie filename entries, with .balign 4 after strings.

In asm/sdata.s, labels were inserted before existing small-data constants and strings, including lbl_804D3EF8 through lbl_804D3F6C and labels near lbl_804D4010, so data.s could reference them symbolically.
The changed-file counts show this was mostly mechanical assembly data cleanup: asm/data.s had 131 hunks, asm/sdata.s 9 hunks, and asm/bss.s 7 hunks.
smart_moves: Split .skip ranges without changing total layout intent, preserving addresses while making interior objects addressable by label.
Converted pointer constants to labels only after adding corresponding labels in bss/sdata/data, improving relocation clarity without requiring C decompilation.
Used .asciz plus .balign 4 for readable string data instead of maintaining opaque .4byte ASCII hex sequences, while preserving alignment-sensitive assembly layout.
Kept the project’s existing anonymous address-label convention, using lbl_XXXXXXXX names for unknown data rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
decomp_lessons: When code or data tables point into the middle of a BSS or small-data region, split the enclosing .skip at the referenced address and create a .global lbl_* at that boundary.
Replacing raw .4byte addresses with labels is a useful intermediate decomp step: it exposes relocation relationships and makes future struct/table identification easier.
Do not over-name unknown data during cleanup. Address labels are appropriate until there is enough evidence for semantic names.
Hex-encoded ASCII in data.s can often be made more searchable and reviewable by converting it to .asciz strings, but alignment must be restored explicitly with .balign 4.
For non-.data cleanup, coordinate label additions across sections: a data.s pointer-table cleanup may require new labels in asm/bss.s or asm/sdata.s first.
naming_conventions: Unknown data and BSS labels use the address-derived form lbl_XXXXXXXX with uppercase hex digits, e.g. lbl_8045A090 and lbl_804D3EF8.
Each newly addressable assembly symbol is paired with a .global declaration immediately before the label.
Raw NULL entries are retained as NULL rather than rewritten to numeric zero where existing style uses NULL.
String labels in this PR still use address labels rather than semantic names, even when string contents are readable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Subdivide large .skip directives into smaller .skip chunks at known referenced addresses while preserving cumulative size and section layout.
Replace .4byte 0x80xxxxxx pointer constants with .4byte lbl_80xxxxxx once the target label exists.
Convert packed ASCII represented as .4byte hex values into .asciz "..." followed by .balign 4 to keep subsequent data aligned.
Expose BSS and sdata interior addresses as globals to support linker relocation/symbol resolution and future data-structure recovery.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context, so no reviewer feedback can be extracted.
searchable_terms: Working through various data labels and finish non-.data labels
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace lbl_8045A090 or neighboring BSS labels with semantic names or structs.
Search for subsequent cleanup of the GmRegendAdventure and GmRegendAllstar THP filename tables.
Look for PRs that further convert lbl_804D3EF8-lbl_804D4130 small-data labels into named strings or enums.
Search for code references to the newly split BSS labels around lbl_80480DC8, lbl_8048542C, lbl_80489A98, and lbl_80490AA8.
